# optical-center — full reference for LLMs

> Bake perceptual optical centering into your SVG icons at build time, with zero
> runtime cost. Source: https://opticalcenter.dev · Repo:
> https://github.com/Grkmyldz148/optical-center · npm: optical-center · MIT.

This file is a self-contained snapshot of the documentation, written as plain
text so language models can ingest the whole project in one request. For the
rendered docs, follow the URLs in each section.

---

## The problem

Geometric center is not visual center. A play triangle (▶) dropped at the
geometric midpoint of its box looks pulled to the left, because its visual mass
sits on the right. optical-center measures where the eye actually wants an icon
to land and shifts the SVG window (the `viewBox`) to put it there, so the
browser ships a flat, pre-computed result.

```
// you write
<svg optical-center viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="M8 5v14l11-7z" /></svg>

// it ships: viewBox rewritten, marker gone, nothing runs in the browser
<svg viewBox="-0.3239 -0.6226 24 24"><path d="M8 5v14l11-7z" /></svg>
```

## The model

The shipped algorithm is `V2 × 0.745` — Phase 1 (N=36, method of adjustment)
globally scaled by the Phase 2 pooled point of subjective equality (N=30, 2AFC).
Algorithm version string: `1.0.0-v2`. See https://opticalcenter.dev/#science and
https://opticalcenter.dev/#research for the study program.

## Two layers

optical-center meets a project at two levels:

1. Automatic — the icons you import. Add the Vite plugin and the icon libraries
   you already use (Iconify, your own SVG files, unplugin-icons, a hand-rolled
   icon map) come out optically centered with no markup. The build finds the
   icon SVG by shape and bakes the shift into the asset. Detection is by SHAPE,
   not by package name. See https://opticalcenter.dev/docs/icons.

2. The directive — explicit control. For icons the build can't reach (a
   component library's render output, a runtime-fetched glyph), or when you'd
   rather center the slot than the asset, write `optical-center: auto` on the
   container. See https://opticalcenter.dev/docs.

The one rule: if the plugin can reach the icon's SVG AT BUILD TIME — as data, a
string, or a file the bundler walks — it is corrected automatically. If the
pixels only exist at runtime, it can't be (you can't optically center what you
can't measure), and you fall back to the container directive, which centers the
slot. Nothing is corrected silently and nothing is skipped silently.

## The rule for the directive

The directive lives on the CONTAINER that wraps the icon, never on the icon
itself (except the inline-SVG pattern, where it sits on the `<svg>`). It
conceptually replaces flex centering: `justify-content: center` +
`align-items: center` tell the browser where the bounding box's middle is;
`optical-center: auto` tells it where the eye wants the icon to land.

Both surfaces compile to the same emitted CSS: `display: flex` on the wrapper,
`margin: auto` on the icon child, plus a `translate` carrying the per-icon
perceptual shift. No `justify-content`, no `align-items`, no
`position: absolute` on the child.

---

## Install

URL: https://opticalcenter.dev/docs/install

Quick path — one command detects framework + package manager, installs the
dependency, and patches the right config file:

```
npx optical-center init                          # interactive
npx optical-center init --integration vite --yes # non-interactive
npx optical-center init ./my-app --dry-run       # report, write nothing
```

Package managers:

```
npm install optical-center
pnpm add optical-center
yarn add optical-center
bun add optical-center
```

### Subpath imports

- `optical-center` — browser-safe core: `getOpticalCenter`, `transformViewBox`,
  `applyTransformToSvg`, types.
- `optical-center/node` — Node-only helpers: `rasterizeSvg` (resvg),
  `transformViewBoxFromSvg`, `sanitizeSvg`.
- `optical-center/cli` — the `optical-center` binary: `init`, `transform`,
  `info`, `analyze`, `clear-cache`, `version`, plus an interactive wizard.
- `optical-center/babel` — Babel plugin for `optical-center="auto"` on JSX
  wrappers and inline `<svg>`.
- `optical-center/vite` — Vite plugin; Babel pass on JSX/TSX + HTML rewrite on
  `index.html`.
- `optical-center/astro` — Astro integration; wraps the Vite plugin and adds a
  post-build sweep over emitted HTML.
- `optical-center/postcss` — PostCSS plugin; reads `optical-center: auto` on CSS
  rules and emits centering + translate.
- `optical-center/tailwind` — Tailwind plugin; registers the `.optical-center`
  utility class.

### Wiring

Vite:

```ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';
import opticalCenter from 'optical-center/vite';
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react';

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [opticalCenter(), react()],
});
```

PostCSS (for the CSS surface; also used standalone for webpack/parcel/etc.):

```js
import opticalCenter from 'optical-center/postcss';

export default {
  plugins: [opticalCenter()],
};
```

Astro:

```js
import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config';
import opticalCenter from 'optical-center/astro';

export default defineConfig({
  integrations: [opticalCenter()],
});
```

Tailwind (the `optical-center/postcss` plugin must run AFTER Tailwind in the
PostCSS chain — Tailwind ships the directive, PostCSS resolves it):

```js
import opticalCenter from 'optical-center/tailwind';

export default {
  plugins: [opticalCenter],
};
```

---

## The five patterns

URL: https://opticalcenter.dev/docs

1. CSS class (canonical, use when in doubt) — put `optical-center: auto` on a
   CSS class, attach the class to a wrapper, drop the icon inside. The PostCSS
   plugin scans JSX, links the class to the icon, emits centering + translate.
   https://opticalcenter.dev/docs/patterns/css-class

2. Tailwind utility — drop the `.optical-center` utility on any wrapper. Same
   end-state as the CSS-class pattern, registered as a Tailwind component.
   https://opticalcenter.dev/docs/patterns/tailwind

3. JSX attribute — inline directive on the wrapper, handled by Babel. No CSS
   file needed; the plugin injects `display:flex` and `translate` as inline
   styles. https://opticalcenter.dev/docs/patterns/jsx-attribute

4. Inline SVG — hand-written `<svg>` inside the wrapper. Babel rewrites its
   `viewBox` at compile time so the shift lives inside the asset, not on the
   element. https://opticalcenter.dev/docs/patterns/inline-svg

5. CSS mask-image — the element IS the icon, mounted via `mask-image` and
   recolored by `currentColor`. PostCSS rewrites the SVG and inlines a `data:`
   URI. https://opticalcenter.dev/docs/patterns/css-mask

The two surfaces, one declaration:

```css
.badge { optical-center: auto; }
```

```tsx
<div optical-center="auto">
  <Play />
</div>
```

---

## Programmatic API

URL: https://opticalcenter.dev/docs/api

Reach for the API when writing a new bundler adapter, generating a static
manifest of pre-computed offsets, running the pipeline in a Worker/Edge runtime
that brings its own rasterizer, or investigating the pipeline in a script.

Browser-safe core:

```ts
import {
  getOpticalCenter,
  transformViewBox,
  applyTransformToSvg,
} from 'optical-center';

const offset = getOpticalCenter(raster);   // { dx, dy, dxPercent, dyPercent }
const result = transformViewBox(svg, raster, offset); // { viewBox, breadcrumb, clipDetected }
const next = applyTransformToSvg(svg, result);        // string with rewritten <svg>
```

Node helpers (wrap the core with the resvg rasterizer):

```ts
import { rasterizeSvg, transformViewBoxFromSvg } from 'optical-center/node';
import { getOpticalCenter } from 'optical-center';

const raster = rasterizeSvg(svgString);
const offset = getOpticalCenter(raster);
// or one-shot:
const result = transformViewBoxFromSvg(svgString); // { viewBox, breadcrumb, clipDetected }
```

Types:

```ts
type Offset = { dx: number; dy: number; dxPercent: number; dyPercent: number };
type ViewBox = { x: number; y: number; w: number; h: number; source: 'attribute' | 'derived' | 'default' };
type TransformResult = {
  viewBox: string;                    // "x y w h"
  breadcrumb: Record<string, string>; // data-* attrs for DevTools
  clipDetected: boolean;              // true if the shift might cut painted pixels
};
```

resvg is a native binary; it stays behind the `/node` subpath so the browser
core never tries to bundle it.

---

## CLI

URL: https://opticalcenter.dev/docs/cli

The CLI operates on SVG files directly (no view of CSS rules or JSX wrappers).
Run the binary bare on a TTY for an interactive wizard; pipes, CI, `--json`, and
`--silent` always get the stable plain-text contract.

Commands:

- `npx optical-center init` — wire optical-center into a project (detects
  framework + package manager, installs, patches config; safe to re-run).
- `npx optical-center transform <input> [output]` — rewrite every SVG in a
  folder. Folder in, folder out.
- `npx optical-center info <svg> [--json]` — inspect one SVG (original viewBox,
  corrected viewBox, offset in px and percent).
- `npx optical-center analyze <folder> [--json]` — top-N report sorted by offset
  magnitude.
- `npx optical-center clear-cache [--all]` — drop the content-addressable cache.
- `npx optical-center version` — package version + algorithm version.

Common flags: `--json`, `--strict` (promote warnings to a non-zero exit),
`--no-cache`, `--cache-dir=<path>`, `--emit-metadata`, `--silent`, `--quiet`.

Exit codes (the contract with CI):

- 0 — success.
- 1 — success with warnings (or `--strict` tripped on a warning). NOTE: npm/yarn
  print a "command failed" banner on any non-zero exit; treat 1 as a soft pass
  unless you used `--strict`.
- 2 — recoverable error (some files failed, run continued).
- 3 — fatal (invalid args, IO failure, aborted run).

Example `info --json` output:

```json
{
  "schemaVersion": 1,
  "command": "info",
  "result": {
    "file": ".../play.svg",
    "originalViewBox": { "x": 0, "y": 0, "w": 24, "h": 24, "source": "attribute" },
    "newViewBox": "-0.3239 -0.6226 24 24",
    "offset": { "dxPercent": 1.3497, "dyPercent": 2.5944, "dx": 1.62, "dy": 3.11 },
    "clipDetected": false
  },
  "version": { "package": "0.2.0-alpha.0", "algorithm": "1.0.0-v2", "schema": 1 }
}
```

---

## Warning codes

URL: https://opticalcenter.dev/docs/warnings

Every adapter emits stable codes so you can grep logs, filter CI, or promote
them to errors with `--strict`.

- OPTICAL_DYNAMIC_SVG — a child/attribute of the SVG is dynamic; can't prove the
  markup at build time.
- OPTICAL_SPREAD_PROPS — a spread attribute might inject the marker at runtime;
  can't statically decide whether to transform.
- OPTICAL_MISSING_VIEWBOX — no viewBox and no width/height pair; no coordinate
  system to shift.
- OPTICAL_VIEWBOX_DERIVED — viewBox derived from width/height; works, but less
  accurate than an explicit viewBox.
- OPTICAL_CLIP_DETECTED — the shifted viewBox may clip painted pixels (paint at
  the edge); inspect output, consider padding the source SVG.
- OPTICAL_RASTERIZE_FAILED — resvg could not parse the SVG (malformed or
  unsupported features).
- OPTICAL_CACHE_WRITE_FAIL — cache dir not writable; compute succeeded, write
  skipped.
- OPTICAL_VERSION_MISMATCH — cached entry from a different algorithm version;
  discarded and recomputed.
- OPTICAL_INPUT_TOO_LARGE — SVG exceeded the per-file size cap; skipped.
- OPTICAL_TIMEOUT — per-file timeout exceeded; skipped.

---

## Status

v0.2.0-alpha. MIT licensed. The core API is stable; subpath adapters (vite,
astro, babel, postcss, tailwind, cli, node) are additive.
